name: Sydney
summary: Welcome to Sydney on Cape Breton Island, Canada — not the famous one in Australia! This is a small, friendly Atlantic port where Celtic fiddle music drifts down the boardwalk and the world-famous Cabot Trail loops through wild highlands full of moose and bald eagles. The first thing you'll spot from the ship is a giant violin taller than a six-storey building.
funFacts: The Big Fiddle on Sydney's boardwalk stands about 18 metres (60 feet) tall — the largest fiddle in the world, built in 2005 to honour Cape Breton music.
The Cabot Trail, a 298-kilometre loop around the island, is regularly named one of the most beautiful drives on Earth by National Geographic and Lonely Planet.
Cape Breton's people mix Mi'kmaq First Nations, Scottish Gaelic settlers, French Acadians and Black Loyalists — you can still hear Gaelic spoken in shops and schools.
Cape Breton Highlands National Park is one of the best places in Canada to spot wild moose, especially along the Skyline Trail at sunrise or sunset.
The Fortress of Louisbourg is the biggest historical reconstruction in North America — a whole 1740s French town where actors in costume bake bread and fire real cannons.
thingsToDo: Pose for a silly photo under the Big Fiddle right beside the cruise pavilion — its strings are thicker than your arm and it sometimes plays Celtic tunes.
Take a half-day taxi or shore-excursion drive along part of the Cabot Trail into Cape Breton Highlands National Park — the full loop is too long for one port day.
Walk a section of the Skyline Trail boardwalk for ocean cliff views and a real chance of spotting moose, eagles or whales far below.
Step back nearly 300 years at the Fortress of Louisbourg, where costumed soldiers march, sheep wander the streets and you can taste 18th-century soldier's bread.
Visit Membertou Heritage Park near Sydney to learn Mi'kmaq stories, see a birch-bark canoe and wigwam, and try a round of the traditional game Waltes.
Listen to a live ceilidh (say 'kay-lee') — a music-and-dance party — somewhere along the boardwalk and try a few quick steps of Cape Breton step-dancing.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in the Mi'kmaq language of Cape Breton's First Nations people

images.alt: A giant blue and brown fiddle sculpture stands tall on the Sydney waterfront beside the cruise pavilion.
The huge Queen Mary 2 cruise ship visits Sydney Harbour on a calm autumn day.
The winding Cabot Trail hugs steep green cliffs above the sparkling blue ocean.
A wooden boardwalk leads down a green headland toward the wide ocean on the famous Skyline Trail.
Stone walls and tall steeples of the historic French Fortress of Louisbourg under a bright blue sky.
